channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Police recover body of 28-year-old varsity student in Gombe
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Gombe Police confirm the recovery of a 28-year-old varsity student’s body from stagnant water. Investigation into the Gombe student death is ongoing.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-recover-body-of-28-year-old-varsity-student-in-gombe/

item.title: Yilwatda hails APC’s progress, promises greater victories ahead
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
APC National Chairman, Nentawe Yilwatda, celebrates a year of significant APC progress, highlighting unity and electoral victories while promising greater
Read More: https://punchng.com/yilwatda-hails-apcs-progress-promises-greater-victories-ahead/

item.title: No kobo was spent on PEAC, PFIPC, Budget office tells Reps
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Budget Office confirms to Reps that no kobo was spent on the controversial PEAC and PFIPC, despite N1.3bn allocation, as conditions for spending were u
Read More: https://punchng.com/no-kobo-was-spent-on-peac-pfipc-budget-office-tells-reps/

item.title: Don’t register again, INEC alerts relocated voters ahead of deadline
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Relocated voters: INEC urges transfer of voter registration ahead of the July 26, 2026 deadline. Don’t register again, simply transfer your details.
Read More: https://punchng.com/dont-register-again-inec-alerts-relocated-voters-ahead-of-deadline/

item.title: Osun closes newly unveiled flyover over structural defect
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Osun State Government temporarily shuts the newly commissioned Lameco Flyover in Osogbo due to a structural defect, sparking political controversy.
Read More: https://punchng.com/osun-closes-newly-unveiled-flyover-over-structural-defect/

item.title: UNESCO condemns killing of Mexican journalist, calls for justice
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
UNESCO condemns the killing of Mexican journalist Josué Martínez Contreras, calling for justice and an end to impunity for crimes against journalists.
Read More: https://punchng.com/unesco-condemns-killing-of-mexican-journalist-calls-for-justice/

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Cammy Devlin revealed the stress he felt at the 2026 World Cup as he looked to make his move to Rangers.

item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: The best Afrobeats albums of 2026 have taken the genre's global reach to new heights, delivering long-anticipated returns, historic collaborations, and bold sonic pivots that have shaped the year's musical narrative. If the singles market has been a fireworks show, the album run of 2026 has been the

item.title: ‘I don’t want problems in my life’ – Femi Kuti declines pictures with Korra Obidi [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Musician Femi Kuti has said he would not want to be involved in an online controversy with celebrity dancer, Korra Obidi. Kuti stated this while humorously declining to take pictures with the dancer after a live concert in Lagos. DAILY POST reports that Femi Kuti’s reaction followed an earlier

item.title: Osun Election: Osun will be the litmus test for INEC, security agencies — Olajengbesi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ikechukwu Nnochiri ABUJA— Ahead of the 2027 general elections, Abuja-based lawyer Mr. Pelumi Olajengbesi on Friday described the Osun State governorship poll, slated for August 15, as a litmus test for both the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) and security agencies in the country.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: …Call for new financing strategy for Africa Africa received $70 billion in foreign direct investment in 2025, representing less than five per cent of the estimated $1.7 trillion in global Foreign direct investment (FDI). Stakeholders stated this at the Africa Social Impact Summit (ASIS) 2026 h

item.title: U.S hits Nigeria, 7 African nations with 12.5% tariff over forced labour claims
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Nkiruka Nnorom The United States has slapped Nigeria and seven other African countries with a 12.5 percent tariff on their exports to America over alleged failure to curb forced labour in supply chains. The action, taken under Section 301 of the US Trade Act of 1974 by the Office of the United St

item.title: Ex-Porn Star Sworn In As Colombian Senator
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A former adult film actress, Deyci Alejandra Omaña Ortiz, has been sworn in as a senator in Colombia. Ortiz, popularly known by her stage name, Amaranta Hank, won a Senate seat in the March elections on a platform advocating rights and protections for workers in the adult entertainment industry. Acc

Akinloye Isiaka Muhammed, has defected to the All Progressives Congress (APC) ahead of the August 15 governorship election in the state. item.title: CBN: Currency in circulation falls to N5.52 trillion in June 2026
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Currency in circulation (CIC) in Nigeria fell to N5.52 trillion in June 2026 from N5.690 trillion in May, reflecting a moderation in cash held within the economy as the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) intensifies efforts to promote digital payments and reduce reliance on physical cash. The post CBN: C

item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Palestinians say villagers came under attack by settlers, while the Israeli military says a Palestinian stole a weapon and opened fire on hikers.

item.description: Niger State Governor, Mohammed Umaru Bago, has insisted that the state deserves a 13 percent derivation from the Federal Government, citing the heavy environmental and economic burden caused by recurrent flooding from the Kainji, Jebba, Shiroro and Zungeru hydropower dams. Bago made this call while

item.title: Former ECOWAS Court President Asante nominated to Ghana’s Supreme Court
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The statement explained that the Ghanaian president made the appointments based on the advice of the National Judicial Council in consultation with the Council of State. The post Former ECOWAS Court President Asante nominated to Ghana’s Supreme Court appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Tribune

item.title: What I track in a day
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Optimizer will be taking a two-week break and will be moving to Wednesdays starting August 12th. Opt in for Opt

item.description: One solution is getting a different job — but that’s not so simple.

item.title: No Kobo Was Spent On PEAC/PFIPC, Budget Office Tells Reps
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The comment came after the CBN said the council's accounts did not receive inflows or remittances. The post No Kobo Was Spent On PEAC/PFIPC, Budget Office Tells Reps appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Pensions: Stop chasing shadows – Atiku fires back at Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: African Democratic Congress, ADC, presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, has waved off the recent insinuation by President Bola Tinubu in respect of the Federal Government pension backlog, describing it as unnecessary ghosting and futile political shadow chasing by an inept APC-led administration.

item.title: Jurgen Klopp Fires Warning to Germany Fans After Taking National Team Job
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: New Germany head coach Jurgen Klopp has issued a strong warning to supporters, saying he will walk away from the job if abuse directed at him extends to his family. Klopp, who has succeeded Julian Nagelsmann as manager of the German national team, made the remarks during his unveiling by the German
